description: baseline: accessibility validation not integrated into docs, critique model, or audit tooling

asi.hypothesis: Baseline should expose whether accessibility is currently a first-class success criterion in the showcase workflow.
asi.interpretation: There is contrast/design-system guidance but no explicit accessibility-tree/keyboard/accessible-name validation in the demo build loop.

description: integrate accessibility validation into showcase goals, routine prompt, critique rubric, and audit warnings

asi.hypothesis: Making accessibility an explicit criterion in the routine, manual critique workflow, typed rubric, and audit tool will close process gaps without touching existing demos yet.
asi.validation: deno fmt normalized docs; deno fmt --check, deno check server.ts, and deno task check passed. Audit now warns on likely static accessibility issues rather than failing current corpus.
asi.important_result: a11y_goal_gaps dropped from 7 to 0; current corpus still has 1801 static issues across 720 pages, so next work should reduce real demo issues rather than add more process text.
asi.anti_overfit_note: The new audit warning is intentionally not the whole accessibility definition; DevTools accessibility-tree and keyboard evidence remain required in docs/routine.

name: Chrome showcase static accessibility issue reduction

description: baseline for reducing objective static accessibility issues

asi.hypothesis: After accessibility process integration, the next useful target is reducing concrete static accessibility findings across demos.
baseline_sample: inputs may be missing labels in v130 part-state-machine and scope-truncation demos
cq-inspector has clickable non-control without role/tabindex
several nesting demos have potentially unlabeled textareas
asi.note: Primary metric changed intentionally from process integration gaps to corpus issue reduction.

description: add aria-labels to generated form controls with no programmatic label

asi.hypothesis: Most static accessibility findings are real unlabeled generated form controls. Adding aria-labels from existing placeholder/name/id values should improve semantics broadly without changing interaction behavior.
asi.result: static_a11y_issues dropped from 1801 to 528. deno fmt --check, deno check server.ts, and deno task check passed. Audit warning page count dropped from 720 to 162.
asi.risk: Large generated HTML touch count (1026 files, 2408 controls). Labels are derived mechanically, so follow-up spot checks with DevTools accessibility tree are still needed; some remaining inputs lack useful id/name/placeholder and need local context.
asi.next_action_hint: Address remaining categories: repeated decision-tree inputs with no useful label source, clickable non-controls, missing image alt, and icon-only buttons.

description: make inline clickable non-controls keyboard-operable and fix scanner attribute parsing

asi.hypothesis: Clickable div/span/etc. with inline onclick need keyboard activation and button semantics; the static scanner also needed to inspect the entire opening tag, not only attributes before onclick.
asi.result: static_a11y_issues dropped from 528 to 341; audit warning page count dropped from 162 to 106. deno fmt --check, deno check server.ts, and deno task check passed.
asi.important_detail: Added role=button, tabindex=0, and Enter/Space onkeydown activation to 187 inline clickable non-controls across 58 files. Corrected measure/audit regex so attributes after onclick are counted.
asi.next_action_hint: Remaining largest class is unlabeled inputs, especially decision-tree style demos lacking useful id/name/placeholder. Next inspect patterns before adding contextual labels.

description: repair malformed aria-label insertions from bulk control labelling

asi.hypothesis: Fixing malformed aria-label insertions caused by regex parsing of self-closing inputs and textarea placeholders containing > should improve correctness and may remove a small number of remaining findings.
asi.result: static_a11y_issues 341 -> 340; audit warning pages 106 -> 105. Checks passed and grep confirmed no remaining '/ aria-label=' or 'aria-label=""' artifacts.
asi.lesson: Bulk HTML edits with regex are risky when attribute values contain '>' or self-closing slash syntax. Future label passes should parse tags more defensively or target narrower patterns.

description: add accessible names to icon buttons and alt text to demo images

asi.hypothesis: Remaining unnamed buttons and images are small, targeted classes where accessible names/alt text can be added from local class/data context without changing behavior.
asi.result: static_a11y_issues dropped from 340 to 294; audit warning pages dropped from 105 to 79. Checks and malformed-insertion grep passed.
asi.details: Added aria-label to 23 empty/icon buttons and alt to 26 img elements across 30 files. Heuristics: carousel dots become Go to slide N, selectedcontent buttons become Open picker, image maps/previews get descriptive alt, otherwise decorative/probe images get empty alt.
asi.risk: Some empty alt choices assume surrounding text explains decorative/probe images; browser accessibility-tree spot checks remain useful.

description: recognize form controls wrapped by label in static accessibility scanner

asi.hypothesis: Many remaining radio/checkbox findings are false positives because controls are wrapped by label elements; the scanner should recognize that native accessible-name pattern.
asi.result: static_a11y_issues dropped from 294 to 3; audit warning pages dropped from 79 to 3. Checks passed.
asi.important_detail: This was a measurement correctness improvement, not a demo semantics change. It avoids pushing unnecessary aria-labels onto controls that already get names from wrapping labels.
asi.next_action_hint: Fix the three remaining image alt findings manually by inspecting context.

description: clear remaining static accessibility findings

asi.hypothesis: The last findings are two real markup issues plus JavaScript/code-sample scanner noise; fixing the markup and ignoring script/pre samples should clear the static audit without weakening real DOM checks.
asi.result: static_a11y_issues dropped from 3 to 0. deno fmt --check, deno check server.ts, deno task check, and malformed aria/alt grep all passed with no warnings.
changes: Percent-encoded the v133 image-map SVG data URI and kept a real outer alt value.
Moved v138 serialization demo aria-label out of the malicious sample value and onto the input itself.
Updated static scanner to ignore script blocks and pre/code examples so XSS payload strings are not treated as page DOM.
asi.anti_overfit_note: The scanner still checks actual page-shell DOM. It ignores examples/payload strings because those are deliberately displayed/generated content, not accessible DOM at load time.

description: document completed static accessibility reduction in playbook

asi.hypothesis: Updating the playbook with completed results helps future agents resume without rediscovering the same path, but should not count as a metric improvement.
asi.rollback_reason: Primary metric was already zero; no further metric improvement possible from documentation-only playbook update.
asi.next_action_hint: Next meaningful work is non-static validation: run representative demos through chrome-devtools-mcp accessibility tree and keyboard checks, or merge/push the completed branch after human review.

description: attempt DevTools accessibility-tree spot check after static audit reached zero

asi.hypothesis: With static accessibility findings at zero, the most valuable next validation is representative Chrome DevTools MCP spot-checking of keyboard behavior and accessibility tree semantics rather than changing more code.
asi.mcp_result: chrome-devtools-mcp connected/listed tools but page/list_pages calls timed out, so no browser or accessibility-tree claim was made.
asi.benchmark_result: Persisted benchmark remains green: static_a11y_issues=0, a11y_goal_gaps=0, audit_strict_errors=0.
asi.rollback_reason: No code change and no primary-metric improvement; this was a validation attempt only.
asi.next_action_hint: Retry DevTools MCP accessibility-tree spot checks when the MCP server responds, starting with v138 serialization-diff and one page with added clickable non-control keyboard semantics. Do not merge solely on static audit results.

description: extend static audit to contenteditable regions and add textbox semantics

asi.hypothesis: The existing static audit missed contenteditable demo regions, which behave like editable textboxes and need explicit role/name semantics for assistive tech.
asi.result: Added textbox roles and accessible labels to 66 contenteditable regions across 42 files; expanded measure/audit to count contenteditable regions missing role/name. The stricter audit found one remaining issue in v145 paste-preview.
asi.validation: deno fmt --check, deno check server.ts, and deno task check passed; audit reports 1 warning under the expanded rule.
asi.anti_overfit_note: This expands the static audit to another real accessibility class instead of gaming the old zero. It does not replace DevTools accessibility-tree validation.

description: correct contenteditable paste preview editor semantics

asi.hypothesis: The only remaining expanded static finding is a real semantic conflict: a contenteditable editor got button semantics from an earlier onclick pass. Changing it to textbox and removing Space/Enter click emulation should restore expected editing behavior and clear the audit.
asi.result: static_a11y_issues 1 -> 0. deno fmt --check, deno check server.ts, and deno task check passed with no warnings.
asi.important_detail: Fixed v145 clipboardchange paste-preview editor: role button -> textbox, removed onkeydown that hijacked Enter/Space. This improves real keyboard/editing semantics, not just the benchmark.
asi.next_action_hint: Search for any other contenteditable with role=button or Enter/Space click emulation before merging; then retry Chrome DevTools MCP spot checks when available.

description: inspect custom role button keyboard support candidates

asi.hypothesis: After contenteditable coverage, the next likely missed static class is custom role=button controls that are focusable but lack keyboard activation.
asi.finding: A grep found five star-rating spans in v130/meter-element-fallback-styles/segmented-meter/index.html, but manual inspection showed the parent starLabels group already has delegated click and keydown handlers that handle Enter and Space via closest('span[data-v]').
asi.benchmark_result: Persisted benchmark remains green: static_a11y_issues=0, a11y_goal_gaps=0, audit_strict_errors=0.
asi.rollback_reason: No code change and no metric improvement; adding redundant inline key handlers would be overfitting and could risk duplicate activation.
asi.next_action_hint: If extending the audit for custom role=button keyboard support, make it delegation-aware to avoid false positives like starLabels.

description: extend static audit to canvas accessibility names and label existing canvases

asi.hypothesis: Canvas demos are another real accessibility class not covered by the current static audit. Since canvas visuals often carry important demo state, canvases should have an accessible name or fallback text unless explicitly hidden.
asi.result: Added aria-labels to 261 canvas elements across 183 demo files and extended the audit/measure scripts to flag unlabeled canvases. Expanded benchmark remains at static_a11y_issues=0. deno fmt --check, deno check server.ts, deno task check, and malformed aria/alt grep passed.
asi.anti_overfit_note: This strengthens the benchmark by adding a genuine accessibility requirement before measuring; labels were derived from local ids/classes and do not hide canvases with aria-hidden.
asi.risk: Canvas labels are broad accessible names, not full textual equivalents of dynamic canvas content. Future DevTools/manual checks should inspect representative canvas-heavy demos for richer adjacent text or live readouts.

description: extend static audit to inline svg accessibility names and hide decorative definitions

asi.hypothesis: Inline SVG graphics are another real accessibility class not covered by the audit. Meaningful SVGs need an accessible name, while hidden symbol/filter definitions should be explicitly hidden from assistive tech.
asi.result: Added role=img/aria-label to 113 SVG graphics and aria-hidden=true to 3 hidden/definition SVGs across 61 files. Extended measure/audit scripts to flag SVGs without a name/title/text or hidden state. Expanded benchmark remains static_a11y_issues=0. deno fmt --check, deno check server.ts, deno task check, and malformed aria/alt grep passed.
asi.anti_overfit_note: This strengthens the audit with a new accessibility class before measuring, and explicitly separates hidden definitions from visible SVG graphics.
asi.risk: Some generated labels are broad (from id/class). DevTools accessibility-tree spot checks remain needed for complex SVG diagrams to ensure the names are useful enough.

description: tighten form-label audit to require matching label for ids

asi.hypothesis: The static audit was too permissive for form controls: it treated any control with an id as labelled if the page had any label element. Requiring a matching label[for=id] or a wrapping label is a more accurate accessibility check.
asi.result: Expanded benchmark remains static_a11y_issues=0 under stricter label matching. deno fmt --check, deno check server.ts, and deno task check passed.
asi.anti_overfit_note: This strengthens the benchmark rather than weakening it; no demo labels were added just to satisfy the old check.
asi.learning: The previous broad aria-label pass already covered controls that lacked explicit matching labels, so the stricter scanner did not surface new issues.

description: enforce button semantics for stateful custom aria controls

asi.hypothesis: Elements using aria-pressed or aria-expanded communicate button-like state and should either be native controls or expose role=button plus keyboard focusability. The current audit missed this real ARIA consistency class.
asi.result: Added role=button/tabindex=0 and Enter/Space activation to six WebAudio use-case picker cards using aria-pressed. Extended audit/measure scripts to flag non-native aria-pressed/aria-expanded elements without role=button and tabindex=0. Expanded benchmark remains static_a11y_issues=0; deno fmt --check, deno check server.ts, deno task check, and a targeted residual scan passed.
asi.anti_overfit_note: This adds an accessibility rule for ARIA state consistency and fixes actual pressable cards, while not requiring brittle static key-handler detection because delegated handlers can satisfy keyboard behavior.
asi.risk: The static rule verifies semantics and focusability, but full keyboard behavior still depends on JS handlers; browser/DevTools spot checks remain useful for representative delegated controls.

description: require valid aria-controls for expanded controls

asi.hypothesis: Controls with aria-expanded expose a relationship to collapsible/menu content, so they should identify the controlled region with aria-controls where the target exists, and aria-controls IDREFs should be valid. The existing static audit did not verify this ARIA relationship.
asi.result: Added aria-controls to 12 expanded controls across popover menus, dropdown menus, a disclosure panel, and focusgroup menus. Added ids to four focusgroup menu panels. Extended audit/measure scripts to flag invalid aria-controls references and expanded controls missing aria-controls. Expanded benchmark remains static_a11y_issues=0; deno fmt --check, deno check server.ts, deno task check, and a targeted residual scan passed.
asi.anti_overfit_note: This strengthens the benchmark with a real ARIA IDREF relationship rule and only links controls to already-existing controlled content; no assertions were weakened.
asi.risk: The static rule verifies relationships exist, not whether runtime expanded state always tracks visibility. Representative menu/disclosure DevTools and keyboard checks remain useful.

description: validate aria IDREFs and page-shell duplicate ids

asi.hypothesis: Accessible names/descriptions and ARIA relationships can silently fail when aria-labelledby or aria-describedby reference missing IDs, and duplicate IDs in the page shell can make IDREF resolution ambiguous. The existing audit only checked that name attributes existed, not that their references were valid.
asi.result: Fixed one broken aria-labelledby reference by replacing it with an explicit aria-label, and resolved a real main-document duplicate id in the Temporal timezone demo by renaming the transition result element and its JS/CSS references. Extended audit/measure scripts to strip srcdoc payloads before page-shell ID checks, flag duplicate page-shell IDs, and validate aria-controls/aria-labelledby/aria-describedby references. Expanded benchmark remains static_a11y_issues=0; deno fmt --check, deno check server.ts, deno task check, and targeted residual scan passed.
asi.anti_overfit_note: The scanner deliberately strips srcdoc attributes before duplicate-ID checks so separate iframe documents are not incorrectly treated as the parent DOM; this avoids false positives while enforcing real page-shell ID integrity.
asi.risk: Regex-based parsing still cannot replace browser DOM inspection for dynamically generated IDs or runtime ARIA changes. DevTools spot checks remain useful for pages with generated controls.

description: prevent aria-hidden on focusable page-shell elements

asi.hypothesis: aria-hidden=true on focusable elements hides reachable UI from assistive technologies. The static audit should catch high-confidence cases where the hidden element itself is focusable, without overreaching into descendant checks that require DOM parsing.
asi.result: Converted two visual-only themed button samples to non-interactive spans and replaced a hidden tabindexed iframe with a titled cross-realm test iframe. Extended audit/measure scripts to flag aria-hidden=true on focusable page-shell elements. Expanded benchmark remains static_a11y_issues=0; deno fmt --check, deno check server.ts, deno task check, and targeted residual scan passed.
asi.anti_overfit_note: The first broad descendant rule produced false positives on decorative SVGs due regex over-capture, so the kept rule is deliberately narrower and high-confidence rather than tuned to hide failures.
asi.risk: aria-hidden ancestors containing focusable descendants are still a real class but need real DOM parsing or DevTools inspection to avoid false positives; this remains a promising future improvement.

description: require accessible names for non-hidden iframes

asi.hypothesis: Non-hidden iframe elements need accessible names so assistive technology users can identify the frame purpose. The static audit previously checked images, SVGs, canvases, and controls but not frames.
asi.result: Added titles to 27 non-hidden iframes across 24 demo pages and moved six titles before long srcdoc attributes so the lightweight page-shell scanner can parse them reliably. Extended audit/measure scripts to flag non-hidden iframes without title/aria-label/aria-labelledby. Expanded benchmark remains static_a11y_issues=0; deno fmt --check, deno check server.ts, deno task check, and targeted residual scan passed.
asi.anti_overfit_note: This adds a standard frame-accessible-name requirement and fixes real unnamed browsing contexts. Titles are semantic descriptions of the frame purpose, not hidden benchmark-only attributes.
asi.risk: Some frame titles are concise and derived from local demo context; DevTools accessibility-tree review can still assess whether complex embedded demos need more descriptive names.

description: require accessible names for progress and meter indicators

asi.hypothesis: Native progress and meter elements expose values to assistive technologies, but without an accessible name users may not know what value is being reported. The audit covered controls and media-like elements but not these value indicators.
asi.result: Added accessible names to 24 progress/meter elements across 12 demo files using local purpose such as battery level, audio level, connection delay, theme match progress, and accent progress. Extended audit/measure scripts to flag non-hidden progress/meter elements without aria-label/aria-labelledby/title or a real label. Expanded benchmark remains static_a11y_issues=0; deno fmt --check, deno check server.ts, deno task check, and targeted residual scan passed.
asi.anti_overfit_note: This adds a standard accessible-name requirement for native value indicators and labels actual visible indicators rather than weakening scanner logic.
asi.risk: Some labels are concise and derived from local demo context; richer surrounding explanatory text may still be needed for complex performance dashboards during DevTools accessibility-tree review.

description: require accessible names for visible audio and video elements

asi.hypothesis: Visible audio and video elements, especially controls and live previews, should have accessible names so assistive technology users can distinguish embedded media demos. The static audit covered iframes and value indicators but not media elements.
asi.result: Added accessible names to 53 page-shell audio/video elements across 47 demo files and extended audit/measure scripts to flag visible non-hidden media elements without title/aria-label/aria-labelledby. Also corrected srcdoc stripping to run before script/pre stripping so iframe payload media is not counted as parent-page DOM. Expanded benchmark remains static_a11y_issues=0; deno fmt --check, deno check server.ts, deno task check, and targeted residual scan passed.
asi.anti_overfit_note: The rule targets visible page-shell media only and deliberately strips srcdoc before scanning, avoiding false positives from separate iframe documents while enforcing real media names.
asi.risk: Some labels are concise and derived from ids/classes; complex media demos may still need fuller surrounding text or captions/transcripts assessed via browser/DevTools review.

description: require accessible names for custom role img elements

asi.hypothesis: Custom visual composites using role=img need an accessible name, just like native image-like elements. The audit covered img/svg/canvas/media but did not enforce ARIA role=img naming.
asi.result: Added role=img accessible-name validation to both audit and measure scripts. No existing page-shell role=img elements were unnamed, so the expanded benchmark remains static_a11y_issues=0. deno fmt --check, deno check server.ts, deno task check, and a targeted residual scan passed.
asi.anti_overfit_note: This strengthens the benchmark with a standard ARIA naming rule without changing demo markup or weakening any checks.
asi.risk: The rule validates only the presence of a role=img name; DevTools/browser review is still needed to judge whether names for complex visual composites are sufficiently descriptive.

description: require accessible names for custom role button controls

asi.hypothesis: Non-native elements with role=button are exposed as controls and should have an accessible name, just like native buttons. The audit covered native buttons and state semantics but did not explicitly validate names for arbitrary custom buttons.
asi.result: Added explicit accessible names to empty color swatch controls, a polygon canvas action target, a popover backdrop close target, and timeline milestone custom buttons. Extended audit/measure scripts to flag non-hidden custom role=button elements without aria-label/aria-labelledby/title or visible text. Expanded benchmark remains static_a11y_issues=0; deno fmt --check, deno check server.ts, deno task check, and targeted residual scan passed.
asi.anti_overfit_note: The rule enforces the same accessible-name expectation as native buttons for real custom controls. Labels describe the actual action or selection target rather than satisfying a keyword-only benchmark.
asi.risk: Regex-based paired-tag parsing can still be imperfect for deeply nested custom controls; browser accessibility-tree review remains the authority for computed names.

description: enforce basic ARIA tab semantics in static audit

asi.hypothesis: Elements with role=tab are custom interactive controls and should expose an accessible name, selected state, and focus management. The audit already covered custom buttons but not tab semantics.
asi.result: Added role=tab validation to both audit and measure scripts: non-hidden tabs must have an accessible name, aria-selected=true/false, and tabindex=0/-1 when not native button/link elements. No existing tabs failed the stricter rule, so expanded benchmark remains static_a11y_issues=0. deno fmt --check, deno check server.ts, deno task check, and targeted residual scan passed.
asi.anti_overfit_note: This strengthens the benchmark with standard ARIA tab requirements without changing demo markup or weakening checks.
asi.risk: The static rule does not validate full tablist keyboard behavior such as arrow-key navigation or panel visibility synchronization; DevTools/browser keyboard review remains needed for tabbed widgets.

description: require accessible names for dialog containers

asi.hypothesis: Visible dialog and alertdialog containers should expose accessible names so assistive technology users know what modal or panel opened. The audit covered many controls and media-like elements but did not validate dialog container naming.
asi.result: Added aria-labels to 66 dialog elements across 42 demo files using local IDs/classes and feature context. Extended audit/measure scripts to flag visible dialog/alertdialog containers without title/aria-label/aria-labelledby. Expanded benchmark remains static_a11y_issues=0; deno fmt --check, deno check server.ts, deno task check, and targeted residual scan passed.
asi.anti_overfit_note: This adds a standard dialog naming requirement and labels actual modal containers with their local purpose rather than weakening the scanner.
asi.risk: Some generated dialog labels are concise. Browser accessibility-tree review is still needed to verify the final computed names and focus behavior for complex dialog stacks.

description: require accessible names for data tables

asi.hypothesis: Visible data/result tables should expose an accessible name via caption or ARIA so screen reader users know what dataset or comparison the table represents. The audit previously covered many widgets but not table naming.
asi.result: Added accessible names to 644 visible data tables across the demo corpus, mostly derived from table ids/classes or header cells, with two targeted manual labels for remaining tables. Extended audit/measure scripts to flag visible non-presentational tables without caption/title/aria-label/aria-labelledby. Expanded benchmark remains static_a11y_issues=0; deno fmt --check, deno check server.ts, deno task check, and targeted residual scan passed.
asi.anti_overfit_note: This adds a standard data-table naming requirement and labels actual table purposes rather than hiding tables or weakening checks. Presentational/hidden tables are skipped.
asi.risk: This is a large corpus-wide metadata pass. Labels are concise and mechanically derived for many tables, so DevTools/manual review should sample representative tables to ensure names are useful, not merely present.

description: require accessible names for fieldset control groups

asi.hypothesis: Visible fieldsets group related form controls and should have a legend or accessible name so assistive technology users understand the group purpose. The static audit checked individual controls but not group naming.
asi.result: Added an accessible name to the one unnamed fieldset in the form lifecycle demo and extended audit/measure scripts to flag visible fieldsets without legend/title/aria-label/aria-labelledby. Expanded benchmark remains static_a11y_issues=0; deno fmt --check, deno check server.ts, deno task check, and targeted residual scan passed.
asi.anti_overfit_note: This adds a standard grouped-control naming requirement and fixes the actual form-control group rather than changing benchmark expectations.
asi.risk: The fix uses aria-label rather than a visible legend to preserve the demo layout; manual review could decide a visible legend is preferable for users who do not use assistive tech.
